channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania prepares national swimming team for Africa Aquatics Championships in Algeria
item.description: Tanzania in gear to send strong national swimming team to Oran, Algeria for the 2026 Africa Aquatics Swimming Championships from May 5 to 10. The 17-member squad combine both seasoned swimmers and you...

item.title: Analysis of Wadagni’s Presidential Win and Its Implications for Benin’s Political System
item.description: Romuald Wadagni, technocrat and former finance minister under Patrice Talon, won the 2026 presidential election in Benin with over 94% of votes. While on the surface this shows overwhelming support, t...

item.title: PCSO Lotto Draws on April 28 Yield No Jackpot Winners
item.description: On Tuesday, April 28, 2026, the Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) reported that no players won the jackpot prizes in the major lotto draws. The Lotto 6/42 had a jackpot of P27,872,450.50, b...

item.title: Jose Mourinho Shows Interest in Returning to Real Madrid Amid Club Manager Search
item.description: Real Madrid dey look their next long-term coach and speculation don start say Jose Mourinho fit return to the club. According to top transfer journalist Fabrizio Romano, Mourinho don already show say ...

item.title: PRC Announces Passers of April 2026 Electrical Engineers and Master Electricians Licensure Exams
item.description: The Professional Regulation Commission (PRC) has officially released the results for the April 2026 licensure examinations for Registered Electrical Engineers (REE) and Registered Master Electricians ...

item.title: Sinner Reaches Madrid Open Quarter-Finals After Early Match Victory
item.description: Jannik Sinner don enter quarter-finals for Madrid Open after e beat British player Cameron Norrie 6-2, 7-5. The match begin 11:00 am, wey rare for the tournament, because e follow special scheduling w...

item.title: Gaza conducts first municipal vote in two decades amid conflict
item.description: Gaza has held its first election in almost 20 years, with voting taking place only in Deir al-Balah. Participation was low, with just under 23 per cent of around 70,000 registered voters casting ballo...

item.title: OpenAI Misses Growth Targets, Triggering AI Stock Decline
item.description: On April 28, 2026, shares of companies linked to OpenAI as partners and investors dropped sharply after a Wall Street Journal report revealed that the ChatGPT creator failed to meet its internal growt...

item.title: Disneyland Introduces Optional Facial Recognition to Streamline Entry
item.description: Disneyland has introduced facial recognition technology at nearly every entrance to Disneyland and California Adventure parks to verify tickets and prevent fraud. While Disney emphasizes that the syst...

item.title: Keir Starmer faces scrutiny over Peter Mandelson's US ambassador appointment and Foreign Office fallout
item.description: In her opinion piece, Guardian columnist Marina Hyde criticises Prime Minister Keir Starmer's handling of the controversial appointment of Peter Mandelson as UK ambassador to the United States. The ar...

item.title: 18-year-old charged with first-degree murder in fatal Windsor stabbing
item.description: Windsor police have identified Hatem Hameed, 18, as the individual charged with first-degree murder following the stabbing death of a 16-year-old boy. The incident occurred on Sunday around 6 p.m. at ...

item.title: Subrata Thakur Addresses Voter Deletions and Community Concerns in Matua Belt
item.description: Subrata Thakur, grandson of P R Thakur and Baroma Binapani Devi, spoke at Thakurbari campus in Thakurnagar, North 24 Parganas, about the Matua community’s history, social dignity, education, and emplo...

item.title: Muli Promises to Hold All Leaders Accountable Over Ukambani's Development
item.description: NLP leader Augustus Muli has openly vowed to challenge any political figure who undermines Ukambani's progress, insisting his remarks are about protecting the region rather than tribal politics. He cr...

item.title: UAE quits Opec amid regional tensions and market concerns
item.description: The United Arab Emirates announced it is leaving Opec and Opec+ effective May 1, 2026, in a significant move that shakes the oil producers’ alliance. UAE Energy Minister Suhail Mohamed al-Mazrouei sta...

item.title: Tshwane Deputy Chief Denies Collusion in Security Contract Allegations
item.description: The suspended deputy chief of the Tshwane Metropolitan Police, Umashi Dhlamini, appeared before the Madlanga commission to refute claims of collusion and tender rigging concerning a R2.9 billion secur...

item.title: Global military spending reaches record $2.9 trillion in 2025 with Australia ranked 17th
item.description: Global military expenditure reached a record high in 2025 according to the Stockholm International Peace Research Institute (SIPRI), rising 2.9 per cent to approximately $2.9 trillion despite a slowdo...

item.title: Government assures Tanzanians safe amid South Africa unrest
item.description: The Tanzanian government has assured its citizens that no Tanzanians have been harmed in the recent xenophobic attacks reported in South Africa. Despite worrying videos circulating on social media, th...

item.title: Lauren Ashley Mastrosa convicted for writing child abuse material but receives community corrections order
item.description: Lauren Ashley Mastrosa, 34, a former marketing executive for a Christian charity, has been convicted for producing, possessing, and distributing child abuse material in her novel 'Daddy’s Little Toy',...

item.title: Foreign Investors Raise Concerns Over Ongoing Land Disputes in Kilifi and Malindi
item.description: Foreign investors, especially Italian nationals living and investing along Kenya's Coast, have expressed concern over escalating land disputes and property losses in Kilifi and Malindi. At the centre ...

item.title: Three Key Canadiens Players Absent from Practice
item.description: Montreal Canadiens faced a setback during their practice session as forwards Cole Anderson and Slafkovsky, along with defenseman Matheson, were absent. While the team did not immediately provide speci...
